text.title: Message Queue Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Message Queue Patterns application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Every consumer is idempotent: same message processed twice produces the same end state. Strategies: idempotency key, db upsert by natural key, check-before-act.
rule.rationale: Brokers deliver at-least-once; non-idempotent consumers double-process under retry, causing visible bugs.

rule.statement: Consumers acknowledge after success, never on receipt. Crashes during processing must retry, not lose the message.
rule.rationale: Auto-ack drops messages on consumer crash; manual ack is the only safe default.

rule.statement: Messages that exhaust retries route to a DLQ. DLQ depth and growth rate are monitored; alerts fire on threshold breach.
rule.rationale: DLQs without alerts accumulate invisibly until the broker disk fills or someone notices a missing feature.

rule.statement: Each consumer declares max retries, backoff strategy (exponential + jitter), and TTL. Infinite retries are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Infinite retries pin worker capacity on poison messages, blocking unrelated work.

rule.statement: Messages carry a `version` field. Consumers branch on version; old versions are accepted for the deprecation window then dropped explicitly.
rule.rationale: Un-versioned schemas force everyone to upgrade simultaneously; versioning lets producer + consumer evolve independently.

rule.statement: Consumers do not emit messages, write to external systems, or commit visible state changes before the ack happens. (Use the outbox pattern for outgoing messages.)
rule.rationale: Pre-ack side effects produce ghost data on crash-after-side-effect-before-ack; the outbox pattern handles this correctly.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
